Scenario.title: The Arcane Door Campaign: Water and Wastewater Systems Intrusion
Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Complex The Arcane Door campaign, conducted by expert threat actors, aims to disrupt water and wastewater systems through a complex series of attacks involving reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Shared DB or directory, Vulnerability, Weak passwords, Improper configuration, Obsolete products, Injection flaws, Custom APIs, Authentication issues
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Phishing, Web, Vulnerability exploit, Malware, Brute-force attack, Social engineering

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 538 - Name: Open-Source Library Manipulation
stage.description: The Arcane Door group begins by gathering information about potential targets using open-source intelligence techniques, focusing on water and wastewater systems.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.description: The group creates living-off-the-land binaries, replacing legitimate ones in shared directories to establish a foothold within the target environment.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2021-25394 [CWE-22] : Microsoft Exchange Server Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
stage.description: The Arcane Door group gains initial access to the target's network by sending spear-phishing emails with malicious attachments exploiting the Microsoft Exchange Server vulnerability.

Scenario.summary: The Arcane Door campaign demonstrates a sophisticated understanding of water and wastewater systems, employing a wide range of tactics and techniques to achieve their goals. Their ability to adapt and evolve their methods poses a significant threat to the industry.
